caption:
The local businesses that win Google search aren't the ones with the biggest budget. They're the ones whose Google Business Profile is actually finished.

I look at profiles across Northern Ontario every week. The pattern repeats: a real business, good work, happy customers, and a profile missing half its free fields. Google can only rank what you give it, and an empty profile gives it almost nothing.

Here are the 4 fields I check on every client. All free.

1. Primary category. This is the highest-weight single field on the profile. Google uses it to decide which searches you are eligible for. Pick the most specific category that is accurate. "Marine Dealer" reaches different searches than "Boat Repair Shop." Most owners set this once at signup and never revisit it.

2. Photos. Google's profile ranking favours active profiles. A new photo every week is a small, steady signal that you are open and operating. It also gives a customer something to look at before they decide to call you or the competitor.

3. Services and Products. Every service you list is another term Google can match you to. It is free keyword space, and most profiles leave the section blank. Filling it in takes an afternoon.

4. Google Posts. A sale, a seasonal update, a new service. They expire after 7 days, so this only works as a weekly habit. Almost nobody does it, which is exactly why it is an edge.

None of this needs a budget. It needs an afternoon and a habit.

When did you last open your Google Business Profile and actually finish it?

#LocalSEO #GoogleBusinessProfile #SmallBusinessMarketing #NorthernOntario #SaultSteMarie
first_comment: Free Google Business Profile visibility check for Northern Ontario businesses: glvmarketing.ca
